mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) reflect consumer spending momentum; rising retail sales signal robust economic activity that supports corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon irrespective of other conditions.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS vint=355,655 (Nov 2008) - sharply below prior year level, confirming recessionary demand collapse
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year rank IC of RSAFS month-over-month change vs 20-day forward SPX return is systematically below 0.03 or opposite sign in both expansion and recession periods.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) crossing above 6.0% from below within a 3-month window marks the onset of a labor market contraction that depresses consumer income and spending, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE vint=6.7 (Nov 2008) - already above 6% threshold, trigger active
failure_condition: No significant negative average 60-day forward return in episodes where UNRATE exceeds the threshold within the prior 3 months, or positive returns in >40% of such episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) indicates a synchronised collapse in manufacturing and labour demand, amplifying recession fears and depressing equity prices over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: Both INDPRO and PAYEMS decline month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vint=106.12 (Nov 2008) vs revised 93.47 signals sharp contraction; PAYEMS vint=136,167 vs revised 135,541 confirms job losses
regime_hint: late-cycle / recession (double-hit from production and labour)
failure_condition: Average 20-day forward return is not more negative when both series decline simultaneously than when only one declines, or the interaction coefficient in a linear model is not statistically significant (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In an economic contraction regime (defined by year-over-year decline in industrial production), the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) has a positive relationship with forward equity returns because low rates during contraction reflect policy accommodation that eventually stimulates recovery. In expansion, the relationship is negative, as low rates signal emerging imbalances. This hypothesis focuses on the contraction regime, predicting that lower FEDFUNDS drives higher forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS vint=0.39 (Nov 2008) - at near zero during deep contraction; INDPRO declining year-over-year
failure_condition: In contraction months, the regression coefficient of 60-day forward returns on FEDFUNDS is not positive and statistically significant, or the sign flips to negative in a majority of contraction episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) falls below 0.5%, the central bank's conventional policy space is exhausted, signalling a liquidity trap that heightens uncertainty about the effectiveness of further stimulus and depresses equity prices over a one-week horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS vint=0.39 (Nov 2008) - trigger active in current environment
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05) in periods where FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%, or more than 40% of such periods produce positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) is a coincident indicator of economic output; a higher level of INDPRO signals stronger aggregate supply and corporate capacity utilisation, which is positively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon as earnings grow.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vint=106.12 (Nov 2008) - well below year-ago level, consistent with recession
failure_condition: Rank correlation of INDPRO level with subsequent 60-day returns is not positive and above 0.05 over rolling 5-year windows, or the relationship is flat in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: During a severe recession, the coexistence of a high unemployment rate (UNRATE > 6%) and an aggressively accommodative monetary stance (FEDFUNDS < 1%) creates a conflicting signal: the labour market distress dominates, leading net negative equity forward returns despite the policy support.
evidence_refs: fact:Both conditions met as of Nov 2008 (UNRATE 6.7%, FEDFUNDS 0.39%)
failure_condition: Average 20-day forward return is not negative in the joint trigger state, or the return is less negative than when only UNRATE > 6% (without low rates).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) rising above their 6-month moving average signals a turnaround in one of the most cyclical sectors of the economy, implying that the worst of the recession is past and forward equity returns over the next 20 trading days become positive.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vint=791 (Oct 2008) - near trough, trigger likely not yet active
failure_condition: No significant positive excess return in the 20 days following trigger activation, or the strategy produces a negative Sharpe ratio in walk-forward testing.
